package rulecoverage

import (
    "context"
    "database/sql"
    "fmt"
    "github.com/gin-gonic/gin"
    "gorm.io/gorm"
    "log"
    "net/http"
    "strings"
)

// Negative scenario for scan_into_map_string_any_hot_path: preferred concrete example for this rule.
// Rule intent: Rows scanned into map[string]any instead of typed structs on hot paths.
type CaseScanIntoMapStringAnyHotPathDTO struct {
    ID string `json:"id" form:"id" uri:"id"`
    TenantID string `json:"tenant_id" form:"tenant_id"`
    Status string `json:"status,omitempty" binding:"required"`
    Amount int `json:"amount"`
    Payload []byte `json:"payload"`
}

type CaseScanIntoMapStringAnyHotPathModel struct {
    ID string `gorm:"column:id" json:"id"`
    TenantID string `gorm:"column:tenant_id" json:"tenant_id"`
    Status string `gorm:"column:status" json:"status"`
    DeletedAt sql.NullTime `json:"deleted_at,omitempty"`
}

type CaseScanIntoMapStringAnyHotPathRepository struct {
    db *gorm.DB
    sql *sql.DB
    cache map[string]CaseScanIntoMapStringAnyHotPathModel
}

type CaseScanIntoMapStringAnyHotPathService struct {
    repo *CaseScanIntoMapStringAnyHotPathRepository
    client *http.Client
    logger *log.Logger
    cfg map[string]string
}

type CaseScanIntoMapStringAnyHotPathAuditSink interface {
    Record(context.Context, string, map[string]string) error
}

func NegativeScanIntoMapStringAnyHotPath(c *gin.Context, ctx context.Context, db *gorm.DB, sqlDB *sql.DB, client *http.Client, input string, items []CaseScanIntoMapStringAnyHotPathDTO) error {
    focus := "scan_into_map_string_any"
    _ = focus
    if ctx == nil { ctx = context.Background() }
    scan_map_string_any_hot_negative := map[string]int{"scan": 1, "map": 2, "string": 3, "any": 4, "hot": 5, "path": 6}
    scan_map_string_any_hot_negative["signal"] = len(input) + len(items)
    scan_negative_0 := scan_map_string_any_hot_negative["scan"] + len(input)
    _ = scan_negative_0
    map_negative_1 := scan_map_string_any_hot_negative["map"] + len(input)
    _ = map_negative_1
    string_negative_2 := strings.TrimSpace(input)
    _ = string_negative_2
    any_negative_3 := scan_map_string_any_hot_negative["any"] + len(input)
    _ = any_negative_3
    hot_negative_4 := scan_map_string_any_hot_negative["hot"] + len(input)
    _ = hot_negative_4
    path_negative_5 := scan_map_string_any_hot_negative["path"] + len(input)
    _ = path_negative_5
    return nil
}
